title: Epic: ATProto Login on Globe Top-Right
description:
## Goal
Add a floating ATProto authentication button to the top-right corner of the globe map view in Green Globe, inspired by Hyperscan's AuthButton pattern. The auth button should be always visible over the globe (not hidden inside the sidebar overlay).

## Context
- Green Globe already has ATProto auth infrastructure: AtprotoProvider (browser OAuth), SignInBluesky dialog, callback page, Account dialog
- But the auth button is currently INSIDE the sidebar overlay panel (DesktopOverlay/SmallerDeviceOverlay), not directly on the globe
- Vercel project (gainforest/green-globe-2) has ATProto env vars that are missing from local .env.local
- Hyperscan's AuthButton is a clean floating button in the top-right with inline login modal + auth'd dropdown — this is the UX model

## Success Looks Like
1. .env.local has all ATProto env vars from Vercel merged with existing vars
2. AtprotoProvider uses env vars (NEXT_PUBLIC_ATPROTO_HANDLE_RESOLVER, NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_ORIGIN) instead of hardcoded values
3. A floating auth button appears in the top-right corner of the globe on all screen sizes
4. Clicking 'Sign in' opens an inline modal for ATProto handle input (like Hyperscan)
5. When authenticated, shows user avatar/name with dropdown (profile + sign out)
6. App builds successfully with 'pnpm build' or 'bun run build'

## Key Constraints
- Use existing useAtproto() hook and AtprotoProvider — do NOT create a new auth system
- Keep existing Privy auth in the sidebar overlay header for backwards compatibility
- The new GlobeAuthButton should be ATProto-only (no Privy)
- Must work on both desktop and mobile

title: Merge Vercel env vars into .env.local for ATProto support
description:
## Files
- .env.local (modify)
- .env.vercel.local (read-only reference)

## What to do
Merge the env vars from .env.vercel.local into .env.local, keeping all existing vars and adding the missing ones.

.env.vercel.local was pulled from Vercel (gainforest/green-globe-2) and contains ATProto-specific vars that .env.local is missing.

Keys ONLY in .env.vercel.local (must be added to .env.local):
- AIRTABLE_GREEN_GLOBE_ORGS_BASE_ID
- AIRTABLE_GREEN_GLOBE_TOKEN
- INVITE_CODES_PASSWORD
- NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_ORIGIN
- NEXT_PUBLIC_ATPROTO_ALLOWED_AUTH_SERVERS
- NEXT_PUBLIC_ATPROTO_HANDLE_RESOLVER
- NEXTAUTH_SECRET
- NEXTAUTH_URL
- PDS_ADMIN_IDENTIFIER
- PDS_ADMIN_PASSWORD
- POSTGRES_URL_NON_POOLING_ATPROTO_AUTH_MAPPING

Keys ONLY in .env.local (keep these, they are NOT in Vercel):
- NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_API_URL
- NEXT_PUBLIC_PRIVY_APP_ID
- NEXT_PUBLIC_PRIVY_CLIENT_ID
- PRIVY_SECRET

Strategy: Read both files. For every key in .env.vercel.local that is NOT in .env.local, append it with its value. For keys that exist in BOTH files, keep the .env.vercel.local value (it's more up to date). Keep the .env.local-only keys.

## Don't
- Delete .env.vercel.local (keep it as reference)
- Commit .env.local or .env.vercel.local to git (they should be in .gitignore)
- Remove any existing keys from .env.local that are not in .env.vercel.local
acceptance_criteria:
1. .env.local contains ALL keys from both .env.local and .env.vercel.local
2. NEXT_PUBLIC_ATPROTO_HANDLE_RESOLVER has a non-empty value in .env.local
3. NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_ORIGIN has a non-empty value in .env.local
4. NEXT_PUBLIC_PRIVY_APP_ID still exists in .env.local (not deleted)
5. Neither .env.local nor .env.vercel.local are staged for git commit
close_reason: b9c3aa6 Merged Vercel env vars into .env.local for ATProto support

title: Update AtprotoProvider to use NEXT_PUBLIC env vars instead of hardcoded values
description:
## Files
- src/app/_components/Providers/AtprotoProvider.tsx (modify)

## What to do
The AtprotoProvider currently hardcodes two values that should come from environment variables:

1. **handleResolver**: Line 141 hardcodes `'https://bsky.social'`. Change to:
   ```ts
   handleResolver: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_ATPROTO_HANDLE_RESOLVER || 'https://bsky.social',
   ```

2. **baseUrl for production**: Line 91 hardcodes `'https://gainforest.app'`. Change to:
   ```ts
   const baseUrl = isProduction
     ? (process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_ORIGIN || 'https://gainforest.app')
     : 'http://127.0.0.1:8910';
   ```

3. **client_metadata.client_id in production**: Line 112 uses `${baseUrl}/client-metadata.json` which is correct (it will pick up the env var via baseUrl).

That's it — just the two env var changes above.

## Don't
- Change the BrowserOAuthClient approach (keep it browser-based)
- Modify the signIn/signOut/restoreSession functions
- Change the OAuth scope or grant types
- Add any new dependencies
- Remove or modify any console.log/debug statements — keep them all as-is
acceptance_criteria:
1. handleResolver reads from process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_ATPROTO_HANDLE_RESOLVER with fallback to 'https://bsky.social'
2. Production baseUrl reads from process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_ORIGIN with fallback to 'https://gainforest.app'
3. All existing console.log and console.warn statements are preserved unchanged
4. File builds without TypeScript errors: npx tsc --noEmit --pretty 2>&1 | grep -c 'AtprotoProvider' should be 0
5. The component still exports useAtproto hook and AtprotoProvider default export
close_reason: 356159c Update AtprotoProvider to use NEXT_PUBLIC env vars for handleResolver and production baseUrl

title: Create floating GlobeAuthButton component and wire into map layout
description:
## Files - src/app/(map-routes)/(main)/_components/GlobeAuthButton.tsx (create) - src/app/(map-routes)/(main)/layout.tsx (modify) ## What to do ### 1. Create GlobeAuthButton.tsx Create a new 'use client' component that provides ATProto login/logout directly on the globe map, floating in the top-right corner. Inspired by Hyperscan's AuthButton (/Users/david/Projects/gainforest/hyperscan/src/components/AuthButton.tsx). **Imports to use:** - `useAtproto` from `@/app/_components/Providers/AtprotoProvider` (existing hook) - `Image` from `next/image` - `Button` from `@/components/ui/button` (existing shadcn/ui) - `Loader2` from `lucide-react` - `useState, useRef, useEffect` from `react` **Component behavior — three states:** **A. Loading** (`!isInitialized`): - Render a small ghost Button with a spinning Loader2 icon - Positioned fixed top-4 right-4 z-[30] **B. Not authenticated** (`isInitialized && !isAuthenticated`): - Render a Button (variant='outline', size='sm') that says 'Sign in' - Styled with glass-morphism: `bg-background/80 backdrop-blur-sm border-border/50 shadow-lg` - On click, toggle `showModal` state (local useState, NOT the stacked dialog system) - When showModal is true, render an inline modal overlay (like Hyperscan): - Fixed full-screen backdrop (bg-black/20 backdrop-blur-sm) — click to close - Centered card (max-w-sm) with: - Title: 'Sign in with ATProto' - Subtitle: 'Enter your Bluesky handle to connect.' - Text input for handle (placeholder: 'alice.bsky.social') - Helper text: 'Just a username? We'll add .bsky.social for you.' - Error message area - Two buttons: 'Cancel' (closes modal) and 'Connect' (calls signIn(handle)) - Use shadcn/ui Input, Button, Label components - On form submit / Connect click: call `signIn(handle.includes('.') ? handle : handle + '.bsky.social')` - The signIn call will redirect the browser (page navigates away), so show 'Connecting...' disabled state **C. Authenticated** (`isAuthenticated && userProfile`): - Render a clickable button showing user avatar + truncated name - Avatar: If userProfile.avatar exists, render next/image (24x24 rounded-full). Otherwise render a div with first letter of displayName/handle - Truncated name: max-w-[120px] truncate - Styled with glass-morphism like the sign-in button - On click, toggle `showDropdown` state - Dropdown (absolute right-0 top-full mt-2): - 'My Profile' link → navigates to `/profile/${userProfile.did}` - Divider - 'Sign out' button → calls signOut() - Use bg-background border rounded-lg shadow-lg - Close dropdown on outside click (useRef + useEffect) **Wrapper div:** All three states wrapped in: ```tsx
{/* content */}
``` ### 2. Modify layout.tsx Current layout (src/app/(map-routes)/(main)/layout.tsx): ```tsx import HoveredTreeOverlay from './_components/HoveredTreeOverlay'; import Map from './_components/Map'; import Sidebar from './_components/Overlay'; export default function MapLayout({ children }) { return (
{children}
); } ``` Add GlobeAuthButton as a sibling: ```tsx import GlobeAuthButton from './_components/GlobeAuthButton'; // ... existing imports export default function MapLayout({ children }) { return (
{children}
); } ``` ## Don't - Use the existing StackedDialog/Onboarding system — this should be a self-contained inline modal - Import or depend on Privy auth — this is ATProto-only - Add any new npm dependencies (everything needed is already installed) - Modify any existing components (Header.tsx, Overlay, etc.) — this is purely additive - Use router.push for the sign-in redirect (signIn() handles the browser redirect internally)
acceptance_criteria:
1. GlobeAuthButton.tsx exists at src/app/(map-routes)/(main)/_components/GlobeAuthButton.tsx
2. It is a 'use client' component
3. layout.tsx imports and renders GlobeAuthButton
4. When not authenticated, a 'Sign in' button is visible in the top-right (fixed position, z-[30])
5. Clicking 'Sign in' opens an inline modal with handle input and Connect button
6. When authenticated, shows user avatar/name with dropdown containing Profile link and Sign out
7. The app builds without errors: pnpm build (or bun run build) completes successfully
8. No new dependencies added to package.json
close_reason: 9da1ac7 Created GlobeAuthButton component with ATProto auth, inline modal, and profile dropdown
